https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MHnYG3Z.title: Chip and Dale decorate for Christmas
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1MHnYG3Z.caption: Chip and Dale string lights, hang ornaments, and turn Animal Kingdom into a Christmas wonderland — chipmunk-style. Produced during my run at Disney, this short leans into the chaos and charm those two have been getting away with since 1943. Short, festive, and exactly as ridiculous as it sounds.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1C_hzcmt.caption: Disney Channel stars Karan Brar and Miranda May loose in the Magic Kingdom for a day — rides, churros, character run-ins, the whole park. Produced for Disney's branded social rollout, edited to play in the scroll without losing the spark of two friends actually having fun. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Urd1yHH.caption: Glow-up merch built for the after-dark crowd at Walt Disney World — light-up ears, LED wands, illuminated drinkware, and the rest of the kit twentysomethings actually post about. Producer credit on the Disney-branded campaign rolling out the Night Mode swag line across parks. Cut moves fast and leans into the neon energy the product was made for.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UkF8q1j.caption: Five Walt Disney World musts curated for the 20-something crowd — produced for Disney's official Happy Place vertical. Tight pacing, on-property shooting, and a hit list built for travelers who want the park beyond the postcard. Hit play for the shortlist.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CGE2rMb.title: Andy Night at Toy Story Land
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CGE2rMb.caption: Fifty guys named Andy take over Toy Story Land after hours at Disney's Hollywood Studios — celebrity Andys included. Produced the night end-to-end: guest wrangling, ride buyouts, capture plan, the whole bit. The footage is exactly as weird and joyful as it sounds.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OfS_lg2.caption: Cache Bunny rolls into Walt Disney World to flip a single wall into an iconic piece of video art. Produced the shoot on the Disney side — locations, crew, clearances, the works — so the artist could focus on the spin. Quick, weird, joyful. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zh3MWLz.title: Adventurer Ed Stafford at Animal Kingdom
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1zh3MWLz.caption: Discovery Channel explorer Ed Stafford trades the jungle for Disney's Animal Kingdom in a behind-the-scenes adventure through one of the most ambitious theme parks ever built. Produced for Disney's in-park content team — part travelogue, part wildlife doc, part love letter to the place where storytelling meets conservation. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VrzQAup.title: WDW Birthday Greeting
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1VrzQAup.caption: Birthday greeting that played for Walt Disney World guests celebrating in the parks — a one-to-one moment turned into a daily broadcast across thousands of birthdays. Produced the piece during my run on the Disney parks creative team, balancing brand warmth with the kind of personal touch that makes a guest feel seen. Six years on rotation, and still the cue that makes a kid lose it at the table. Hit play.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/10h6W7Eq.caption: Sarge drills Orlando City SC like a fresh squad of green army men in this Disney/Toy Story Land crossover spot — boots, barked orders, and big-league branding collision. Produced inside Disney's in-house team, the piece threads the parks IP into a real MLS partnership without losing the joke. Hit play to see pro athletes take orders from a plastic sergeant.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vDKPf3Z.caption: A behind-the-scenes promo with Sean Giambrone and Olivia Rodrigo riding the Mad Tea Party to hype the Disney Christmas Parade — produced on the ground at Disneyland. Tight turnaround, two young leads, one spinning teacup, and a holiday tune-in to land. The kind of segment where the magic only works if the day runs like clockwork.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1LdSigiL.caption: An April Fools' bit dreamed up inside Walt Disney World — snack plus ears equals snears, the must-have park accessory nobody asked for. Directed and produced as a straight-faced product launch, complete with hero shots, testimonials, and the kind of earnest voiceover that sells the joke. Watch how long it takes before you realize it's not real.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wfesqgo.caption: Disney Channel icon Johnny Tsunami trades the board for a cubicle in this Oh My Disney Vine — six seconds of premium nostalgia bait built for the scroll. Directed and produced during my run on the Oh My Disney social team, where the brief was simple: make the internet feel something fast.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wTfxHKI.caption: A stop-motion Tsum Tsum parade winding through a miniature Disneyland — every float, lamppost, and crowd reaction built and shot frame by frame. Directed and produced for Disney's social channels, where the goal was to make scrolling thumbs stop. Plush characters, real sets, zero CGI shortcuts.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iTlo9sG.caption: Behind-the-scenes look at how an Olaf Disney Infinity figure goes from raw sculpt to retail shelf, cut for the Unboxing Frozen series. Tight pacing across modeling, paint, and packaging beats — the kind of process reel that makes you want to see every figure get the same treatment. Edited during my run on Disney-branded content.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1iQRpASR.caption: Nature-doc parody following a Goofy tsum tsum through the wild — until a very real cat enters the frame. Cut as a straight-faced wildlife episode, complete with hushed narration beats and slow push-ins, then derailed by a predator with claws. Part of the Tsum Tsum Kingdom series I edited during my Disney years. Press play before the chase starts.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1z7V5hY6.title: Making a Sled Dolly for a Marvel Film
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1z7V5hY6.caption: Built a custom sled dolly for a Marvel-branded film shoot — a quick build doc from my personal YouTube channel showing the rig coming together, the problem it solved on set, and how it held up under a real production day. Shot, narrated, and cut by me between gigs at Disney. Practical filmmaking, no fluff.

https://www.instagram.com/artios_sugarhi.title: The Little Mermaid - Promo
https://www.instagram.com/artios_sugarhi.caption: Cut a hype piece for a high school staging of The Little Mermaid — built to fill seats, shared across the school's social channels. Produced and edited the spot, pulling rehearsal footage and cast moments into something that earns a click. Hit play to see how the show sells itself.

Behind the scenes at Tree Sound Studios as the high school cast tracked songs from Beauty and the Beast for the school's stage production. Produced and edited the doc piece for the school's social channels — close-mic vocals, headphone takes, the look on a kid's face when they nail the chorus. Real studio, real nerves, real performances.

https://www.instagram.com/jacksterner/re.title: Big Fish Promo
https://www.instagram.com/jacksterner/re.caption: Cut a high school theater promo for Big Fish — produced and edited the spot to drive ticket sales and build buzz around the show. Pulled rehearsal footage and cast moments into something that actually felt like the musical, not a slideshow. Made for the school's social channels, where it ran ahead of opening night.
